purpose.statement: Describe how live issues are surfaced, triaged, and escalated so that people know where concerns go and who is involved.

template_block.name: scope
template_block.title: Scope
template_block.statement: This model applies to:

checklist.entry: concerns raised by staff, users, or partners

template_block.statement: It should be reviewed when:

checklist.entry: escalation paths are unclear or contested

template_block.name: what-counts-as-a-live-issue
template_block.title: What counts as a live issue
template_block.statement: Describe the types of situations that may require attention.
Examples (describe, do not prescribe):

checklist.entry: sustained increase in user contact or complaints

template_block.statement: Record uncertainty explicitly if thresholds are unclear.

template_block.name: signals-and-detection
template_block.title: Signals and detection
template_block.statement: Describe how issues become visible, without mandating tools or actions.
Notes:

checklist.entry: Absence of a signal does not imply absence of an issue.
Signal reliability and coverage should be stated if known.

template_block.name: triage-and-initial-handling
template_block.title: Triage and initial handling
template_block.statement: Describe how issues are initially handled, not how they are resolved.

checklist.entry: How issues are categorised (if at all):
Known limitations or ambiguities in triage:

template_block.statement: Do not include procedural instructions.

template_block.name: operational-escalation
template_block.title: Operational escalation
template_block.statement: Describe when and how issues are escalated within operations.

checklist.entry: Escalation route (role or team, not individuals):

template_block.name: governance-escalation
template_block.title: Governance escalation
template_block.statement: Describe when issues move beyond operational handling.

checklist.entry: Decisions that may be taken at this level:
Decisions explicitly not taken at this level:

template_block.name: interfaces-with-other-teams-or-organisations
template_block.title: Interfaces with other teams or organisations
template_block.statement: Describe boundaries and handoffs, not responsibilities for outcomes.

template_block.name: out-of-hours-and-continuity-considerations
template_block.title: Out‑of‑hours and continuity considerations
template_block.statement: Describe what changes outside normal working patterns, if applicable.

checklist.entry: Reduced coverage or delayed response expectations:
Known risks introduced by out‑of‑hours arrangements:

template_block.name: known-gaps-and-uncertainties
template_block.title: Known gaps and uncertainties
template_block.statement: Explicitly record:

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine‑Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: ```yaml lint: mode: hard-fail

escalation_model_integrity: description: > Live support and escalation models must describe how issues move, not how they are fixed, nor who is at fault. rule: Reject outputs that imply solutions, performance judgement, or blame.
no_operational_instruction_language: description: Must not include step-by-step instructions or commands. disallowed_terms:
severity: error
no_sla_or_target_language: description: Must not define SLAs, SLOs, targets, or response times unless explicitly provided. disallowed_terms:
severity: error
no_blame_or_fault_language: disallowed_terms:
severity: error
escalation_paths_required: description: Must describe at least one operational and one governance escalation path, or explicitly state Unknown. severity: error
ambiguity_and_uncertainty_required: description: Areas of unclear thresholds, ownership, or handoff must be explicitly recorded. severity: error
role_based_descriptions_only: description: Escalation paths must reference roles or teams, not named individuals. severity: error
tone: allowed:
disallowed:
severity: error ```
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
